agent1_snippets.content: In patients with complex or poor-risk cytogenetics, secondary, or therapy-related AML, FLAG is the preferred regimen. agent1_snippets.title: Acute Myeloid Leukemia -- Treatment / Management -- Relapsed/Post-HSCT Acute Myeloid Leukemia
agent1_snippets.content: Several agents are available for patients experiencing relapsed AML with specific mutations identified through molecular sequencing techniques. Fms-like tyrosine kinase 3 (FLT3) inhibitors, such as gilteritinib, may be recommended and have demonstrated higher complete remission rates than salvage chemotherapy in this patient population. [29] Patients with IDH1 mutations, either in the relapsed setting or among older populations, unfit individuals unsuitable for induction therapy, should be offered ivosidenib or olutasidenib. agent1_snippets.content: NPM1 mutations in acute myeloid leukemia: Mutations in the NPM1 gene are generally associated with a favorable prognosis in AML, particularly when not accompanied by FLT3-ITD mutations. agent1_snippets.title: Leukemia -- Histopathology -- Acute Leukemia
agent1_snippets.content: In acute leukemia, the peripheral blood or bone marrow is characterized by more than 20% blasts. However, regardless of the blast percentage, patients with t(8;21)(q22;q22), RUNX1-RUNX1T1, inv(16)(p13.1q22) or t(16;16)(p13.1;q22), CBFB-MYH11 or t(15;17)(q24.1;q21.1), PML-RARA, are considered and treated as acute leukemia. There is usually increased cellularity noted on bone marrow biopsy that is packed with blasts and a variable number of granulocytic or monocytic cells and erythroid precursors. Traditional markers included in the evaluation are CD7, CD11b, CD13, CD14, CD15, CD16, CD33, CD34, CD45, CD56, CD117, HLA-DR. Also, either peripheral smear or bone marrow aspirate is sent for a mutation panel of multiple genes with therapeutic and prognostic implications, such as ASXL1, CEBPA, DNMT3A, FLT3, IDH1, IDH2, NPM1, RUNX1, and TP53, to mention a few.

agent1_snippets.title: Acute Myeloid Leukemia -- Treatment / Management -- Induction Therapy—Regimen Selection
agent1_snippets.content: Induction therapy represents the standard of care for all patients with AML, and decisions regarding the selection of induction chemotherapy should not be solely based on age. In younger patients (typically aged 70 or younger), individuals who are deemed fit (ECOG performance status scale ≤2), and those with de novo AML without complex (ie, ≤3 abnormalities) or poor-risk characteristics, the preferred regimen is the "7+3" protocol. This regimen involves a continuous infusion of cytarabine (ie, Ara-C) for 7 days combined with anthracycline administration on days 1 to 3. When using daunorubicin, a dosage of 90 mg/m 2 /d has been associated with improved overall survival. [20]

agent1_snippets.title: Acute Myeloid Leukemia (Nursing) -- Medical Management -- Consolidation Therapy
agent1_snippets.content: After achieving CR with induction therapy, consolidation therapy is initiated with high dose cytarabine, called HiDAC and hematopoietic cell transplantation (HCT). HCT is preferred in individuals with less than 60 years of age with intermediate or unfavorable prognosis. If a donor is available, then allogenic HCT is preferred over autologous HCT. They should be Monitored for signs or symptoms of acute or chronic graft versus host disease (GVHD). agent1_snippets.title: Laboratory Evaluation of Acute Leukemia -- Diagnostic Tests -- Cytogenetic Analysis
agent1_snippets.content: Prognostic significance of cytogenetic abnormalities : Cytogenetic abnormalities have a significant impact on determining the prognosis of patients with acute leukemia. Certain abnormalities are associated with favorable outcomes, whereas others indicate poor prognoses (see Table 2 ). [49] Favorable prognosis: Patients with t(8;21) AML, inv(16) AML, or t(12;21) ALL generally have a better prognosis and respond well to standard chemotherapy. Intermediate prognosis: Patients with normal karyotype AML or those with non-recurrent cytogenetic abnormalities have an intermediate prognosis. Poor prognosis: Patients with complex karyotype AML, monosomal karyotype AML, or t(9;22) ALL are at high risk for treatment failure and relapse, necessitating more intensive therapeutic approaches. [49]

agent1_snippets.content: Treatment options vary depending on patient-specific factors, and hematopoietic stem cell transplant remains the only curative therapy. Although the administration of multiagent induction chemotherapy can achieve complete remission, allogeneic stem cell transplantation is the only established curative therapy. In addition, this activity emphasizes the crucial role of the interprofessional healthcare team in providing care for patients affected by this condition.

agent1_snippets.title: Leukemia Cutis -- Medical Oncology
agent1_snippets.content: Initial treatment of myeloid leukemia is based on age, history of prior myelodysplastic syndrome or cytotoxic chemotherapy, and performance status. The use of cytogenetics and molecular markers gained through next-generation sequencing is necessary for risk stratification and can guide upfront frontline therapy. Several molecular markers such as FLT3, NPM1, CEBPA, IDH, And other mutations are important for risk assessment. However, although karyotyping and molecular markers are robust predictors of disease-free survival, induction chemotherapy is often initiated before this information is available. Standard induction regimens have as their basis the backbone of cytarabine in combination with anthracycline popularly known as the 7+3 regimen. The therapy intends to reduce the leukemic burden and restore normal hematopoiesis. For people that are found to be FLT3 mutated, midostaurin, an FLT3 inhibitor to both ITD and TKD mutations, is now added to standard chemotherapy as part of frontline treatment based on the RATIFY Alliance Trial.

agent1_snippets.title: Acute Myeloid Leukemia -- Pathophysiology
agent1_snippets.content: Runt-related transcription factor ( RUNX1 ) is an essential component of hematopoiesis and is also known as AML1 protein or core-binding factor subunit alpha-2 ( CBFA2 ). RUNX1 is located on chromosome 21 and is frequently translocated with the ETO (Eight Two One) /RUNX1T1 gene located on chromosome 8q22, creating an AML-ETO or t(8;21)(q22;q22) AML, which is seen in about 12% of AML cases. These mutations, commonly associated with trisomies 13 and 21, show resistance to standard induction therapy.

agent2_snippets.content: Ponatinib, ponatinib hydrochloride, and asciminib are therapeutic agents that may be used to treat chronic myeloid leukemia (ابيضاض الدم النقوي). agent2_snippets.title: UMLS -- Run 19 -- Level 4 -- Community 2916
agent2_snippets.content: Dasatinib anhydrous and imatinib are both classified as BCR‑ABL inhibitors. As BCR‑ABL inhibitors, these agents specifically target the BCR‑ABL fusion protein, a constitutively active tyrosine kinase that drives the development of chronic myeloid leukemia and certain acute lymphoblastic leukemias. By inhibiting BCR‑ABL activity, dasatinib anhydrous and imatinib disrupt downstream signaling pathways, reducing malignant cell proliferation and providing therapeutic benefit in BCR‑ABL–positive hematologic malignancies. Their shared classification highlights a common mechanism of action, while differences in potency, spectrum of activity, and side‑effect profiles inform clinicians’ choices of targeted therapy for patients with BCR‑ABL–driven disease.

agent2_snippets.title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 1 -- Community 214
agent2_snippets.content: Bruton's tyrosine kinase (BTK) inhibitors are classified as protein kinase inhibitors with antineoplastic and immunomodulating activity. Within this class, ibrutinib and zanubrutinib are members; ibrutinib is used to treat chronic lymphocytic leukemia (سرطان الخلايا الليمفاوية المزمن). Epidermal growth factor receptor (EGFR) inhibitors also belong to the broader category of protein kinase inhibitors, antineoplastic and immunomodulating agents. Canertinib dihydrochloride and margetuximab are specific EGFR inhibitors. Other protein kinase inhibitors listed in the Anatomical Therapeutic Chemical (ATC) classification include cabozantinib, gilteritinib, and midostaurin. Gilteritinib and midostaurin are indicated for the treatment of acute myeloid leukemia (سرطان الدم النخاعي الحاد), while cabozantinib is a member of this group with oncologic applications. Collectively, these agents illustrate how diverse protein kinase–targeting drugs—spanning BTK inhibitors, EGFR inhibitors, and other kinase inhibitors—are integrated into cancer therapy, each targeting specific malignancies and contributing to personalized patient care.

agent2_snippets.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 1286
agent2_snippets.content: Imatinib is a BCR‑ABL inhibitor, meaning it belongs to the class of drugs that specifically target BCR‑ABL fusion proteins. BCR‑ABL fusion proteins serve as the molecular target for BCR‑ABL inhibitors; they are oncogenic drivers created by the Philadelphia chromosome translocation and are central to the pathogenesis of chronic myeloid leukemia and certain acute lymphoblastic leukemias. By binding to and inhibiting these fusion proteins, imatinib and other BCR‑ABL inhibitors can suppress the abnormal signaling that promotes uncontrolled cell proliferation, thereby providing an effective targeted therapy for patients with these malignancies.

agent2_snippets.title: UMLS -- Run 19 -- Level 1 -- Community 214
agent2_snippets.content: The community describes blood cancer (ابْيضَاضُ الدَّم) as a group of leukemic disorders comprising lymphoid leukemia (ابيضاض الدم اللمفاوي) and myeloid leukemia (ابيضاض الدم النقوي), which correspond to chronic lymphocytic leukemia (سرطان الخلايا الليمفاوية المزمن) and acute myeloid leukemia (سرطان الدم النخاعي الحاد), respectively. Targeted therapy relies heavily on protein kinase inhibitors, antineoplastic and immunomodulating agents. BCR‑ABL inhibitors—dasatinib, dasatinib anhydrous, imatinib, imatinib mesylát, ponatinib, ponatinib hydrochloride, and asciminib—block the BCR‑ABL tyrosine kinase; the former four are used for lymphoid leukemia, whereas ponatinib, ponatinib hydrochloride, and asciminib are indicated for myeloid leukemia. Bruton's tyrosine kinase inhibitors ibrutinib and zanubrutinib also belong to this class, with ibrutinib approved for chronic lymphocytic leukemia. Additional protein kinase inhibitors include EGFR inhibitors canertinib dihydrochloride and margetuximab, as well as other ATC‑listed agents cabozantinib, gilteritinib, and midostaurin; gilteritinib and midostaurin are employed in acute myeloid leukemia. Corticosteroids such as dexamethasone acetate (used for blood cancer and septic shock), dexamethasone phosphate, prednisolone sodium metasulphobenzoate, prednisolone sodium phosphate, and prednisolone tebutate demonstrate efficacy in hematologic malignancies. Chemotherapeutic agents irinotecan hydrochloride and pipobroman are also utilized. Contraindicated interventions include infection with human herpesvirus 1 and oncolytic viral therapy talimogene laherparepvec, which may exacerbate the disease. Collectively, these agents illustrate the role of kinase‑targeted and immunomodulatory drugs in the comprehensive management of leukemic disorders.

Bruton’s tyrosine kinase (BTK) inhibitors, also protein kinase inhibitors with antineoplastic and immunomodulating activity, include ibrutinib and zanubrutinib; ibrutinib is approved for chronic lymphocytic leukemia. BCR‑ABL inhibitors—dasatinib, dasatinib anhydrous, imatinib, ponatinib, ponatinib hydrochloride, and asciminib—share the mechanism of inhibiting the BCR‑ABL tyrosine‑kinase fusion protein and are assigned to specific subtypes: dasatinib, its anhydrous form, and imatinib are indicated for lymphoid leukemia, whereas ponatinib, ponatinib hydrochloride, and asciminib are indicated for myeloid leukemia. Additional anti‑leukemic agents include the corticosteroid derivative kortizon acetát, dexamethasone phosphate, the topoisomerase‑I inhibitor irinotecan hydrochloride, the antimetabolite methotrexate sodium, the alkylating agent pipobroman, and several prednisolone salts (prednisolone sodium metasulphobenzoate, prednisolone sodium phosphate, prednisolone tebutate). agent2_snippets.title: UMLS -- Run 11 -- Level 1 -- Community 81
agent2_snippets.content: Blood cancer (ابْيضَاضُ الدَّم) encompasses leukemias, including lymphoid (ابيضاض الدم اللمفاوي) and myeloid (ابيضاض الدم النقوي) forms, with specific subtypes such as chronic lymphocytic leukemia (سرطان الخلايا الليمفاوية المزمن) and acute myeloid leukemia (سرطان الدم النخاعي الحاد). Dexamethasone acetate additionally serves in the management of septic shock. Conversely, viral agents such as human herpesvirus‑1 and the oncolytic therapy talimogene laherparepvec are contraindicated in patients with blood cancer.
